weather.tip: Drizzly evening at 21.6°C with a 93% chance of more rain overnight — carry an umbrella, avoid known waterlogging spots, and build in extra time if you're heading out for dinner or errands.

around.headline: Bengaluru woman dies by suicide after alleged dowry harassment; husband, father-in-law arrested
around.summary: Police have arrested a woman's husband and father-in-law after she died by suicide following what she described as months of dowry-related harassment. If you or someone you know is facing this kind of pressure, Karnataka's women's helpline (181) and the local police station both take these complaints.

around.headline: Decomposed body of a paramedical/nursing student found in her Bengaluru room after three days
around.summary: A young woman's body was discovered only after neighbours noticed a foul smell, days after she was last seen. It's a reminder to check in on flatmates, neighbours, or family living alone if they go uncharacteristically quiet for more than a day.

around.headline: CBI files chargesheet against a real estate firm for cheating home buyers
around.summary: The CBI has chargesheeted a real estate company over allegations of cheating buyers who paid for flats. Before booking with any builder, verify the project's RERA registration and avoid large cash payments outside an escrow-backed channel.

around.headline: Namma Metro: playing loud music or filming reels can now draw a Rs 2,500 fine
around.summary: BMRCL has raised penalties for loud music, shouting, and filming reels on Metro premises under the Jan Vishwas Act amendments. Keep phones on silent and save the reel-shooting for outside the paid area.

around.headline: 30% of teachers in Karnataka govt schools are on SIR duty, hitting classroom lessons
around.summary: A report today says roughly a third of government-school teachers have been pulled onto Special Intensive Revision (electoral roll) duty, disrupting academic schedules. Parents with kids in govt schools should expect some lesson slippage until the SIR exercise wraps up.

around.headline: US renews strikes on Iran as Strait of Hormuz tensions push toward a 'worst-case scenario'
around.summary: The US has carried out fresh strikes on Iran-linked targets after an attack on its personnel, and shipping through the Strait of Hormuz is increasingly disrupted. A prolonged standoff there tends to feed through to global oil prices, and eventually to petrol and cab fares at home.

events.name: Art Bengaluru Collective — 11th annual art exhibition
events.note: A citywide showcase of contemporary art now in its 11th edition — a good pick if you want a low-key gallery outing this week.

events.name: Tarangini 2026, headlined by Ustad Amjad Ali Khan
events.note: The renowned sarod maestro headlines this Bengaluru concert — worth booking ahead if you're into classical music.

events.name: 7th Arjan Singh Memorial Hockey Tournament
events.note: A multi-day hockey tournament has kicked off in the city — worth catching if you're near the venue and enjoy live sport.

street_smart.title: A city bus is inching into your lane at a junction, and you're on a two-wheeler or auto right beside it. What do you do?
street_smart.based_on: Real case reported today: a BMTC electric bus rammed an autorickshaw near K R Circle, killing the 60-year-old auto driver — reigniting warnings about riding alongside buses at junctions (Deccan Herald / The Times of India / The Hindu).
street_smart.situation: You're riding a two-wheeler or driving an auto in slow-moving traffic near a junction, and a city bus starts merging or turning into your lane. There isn't much space, and it doesn't look like the driver has seen you.

choices.text: Speed up to get past the bus and clear the gap before it fully merges.
choices.feedback: This can work if there's a genuinely clean gap, but a driver approaching a junction is focused on pedestrians and oncoming traffic, not a fast-closing gap on their blind side — misjudging that gap by even a second is how today's fatal collision near K R Circle happened.

choices.text: Slow down and drop back so the bus completes its turn or merge before you move up.
choices.feedback: Correct. Buses have large blind spots, especially on the side closest to two-wheelers and autos, and their drivers are already managing a lot at a junction. Falling back costs you a few seconds; squeezing past costs you the room to react if the bus doesn't stop in time.

choices.text: Stay right where you are and honk to make sure the driver knows you're there.
choices.feedback: Horns get lost in junction noise, and standing still in a bus's blind spot doesn't make you safer — you're betting your safety on the driver noticing a sound instead of removing yourself from the danger zone, which is exactly the position today's victim was in.

choices.text: Cut across to the far side of the road to get well clear of the bus altogether.
choices.feedback: This gets you out of the bus's blind spot, but weaving across lanes to do it puts you in the path of vehicles you're not watching for. Dropping back achieves the same safety without crossing traffic.

takeaways: Buses and trucks have large blind spots on the side closest to two-wheelers and autos — never assume the driver has seen you, especially near a junction.
When a heavy vehicle is merging or turning into your lane, drop back rather than racing to beat it through the gap — losing a few seconds costs less than losing your room to react.
If you witness reckless driving by a bus or heavy vehicle, note the vehicle number if it's safe to do so and report it via 112 — specifics make repeat-offender routes easier to act on.

quiz.q: Under revised BMRCL penalties following Jan Vishwas Act amendments, what fine can you now get for playing loud music or filming reels on a Namma Metro train?
options: Just a verbal warning, no fine
quiz.why: The higher penalty is part of a broader shift where many minor civic offences moved from criminal complaints to fixed monetary fines under the Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Act.

quiz.q: Roughly what share of teachers in Karnataka's government schools are currently away on Special Intensive Revision (SIR) poll duty, per a report today?
quiz.why: SIR is the door-to-door re-verification of electoral rolls that's also why the Supreme Court extended Bengaluru's civic poll deadline to December.

quiz.q: How many people are feared dead after flash floods hit Jammu & Kashmir following heavy rain reported today?
quiz.why: Flash floods are common in Himalayan states during the July–August monsoon peak, when saturated hillsides can give way with little warning.

quiz.q: Bengaluru's municipal water supply is drawn mainly from which river, then pumped roughly 100 km and uphill to the city?
quiz.why: That long uphill pumping is a big reason Bengaluru's water costs and shortages are so sensitive to rainfall in the Cauvery basin, not just local rain.

quiz.q: CBI today chargesheeted a real estate firm for cheating buyers. What does RERA — the law meant to protect home buyers — stand for?
quiz.why: RERA requires builders to register projects and often escrow a share of buyer payments, which is why checking a project's RERA registration before booking is a basic safeguard.
